title: Jovito R. Salonga, my guru and surrogate father
sub_topics: divestment of financial interests upon entering government
dissolving private law firm upon Supreme Court appointment

stances.claim: Salonga was CJ's guru, surrogate father, and lifelong role model — teaching by example rather than words the values of integrity, prudence, and fairness.
Law cannot be separated from life — it should be used as a brick in building the social edifice and as a means to fulfill the deepest aspirations of man.
stances.rhetorical_move: transmission of mentor's teaching framed as own credo

stances.claim: Public servants entering government should divest financial interests and dissolve private law firms — not because law strictly requires it, but as a personal ethic of being beyond suspicion.
stances.rhetorical_move: ethical norm extracted from mentor's example + personal emulation (his own dissolution of Panganiban, Parlade, Benitez and Africa upon Supreme Court appointment)

stances.claim: Religious difference (Protestant vs Catholic) need not diminish deep friendship, provided what is discussed is what binds rather than what separates.

institutions: National Union of Students of the Philippines
Panganiban, Parlade, Benitez and Africa (law firm)

signature_phrases: Let me add my humble voice
Thus did I find the guru
I try to echo, even if unworthily and faintly
You now have formidable wings, go and soar the limitless sky!
register_markers: Let me add my humble voice
decision_framework_signals: frames professional ethics as 'beyond suspicion' standard exceeding legal minimum — derived from Salonga's example
frames mentor-disciple transmission as the mechanism by which legal values propagate across generations
cross_references: log on to cjpanganiban.com and click 'speeches' for full eulogy
notable_anecdotes: 1956 FEU law strike — Salonga, 36, holding rambunctious crowd spellbound for 30 minutes
wading knee-deep floodwaters from FEU Hospital to UE during the 1960 bar exams
American Embassy denying his travel grant to Yale due to student activism
Salonga consoling him with 'Come, join my law office and I will teach you what I learned from UP, Yale and Harvard'
Salonga's blessing on his departure: 'You now have formidable wings, go and soar the limitless sky!'
Salonga citing Romans 8:28 to lift his spirits during repeated JBC screening failures
